perturbation.original_text: 7.5 All credits will be handled as separate financial transactions from the original invoice, and will be evidenced by a written credit request submitted to Tripath. Distributor will not reduce any payment to Tripath to account for anticipated or unauthorized credits (except for short shipments and non-receipt of Products), either at the time of the payment of original invoice, or at the time of providing a credit request. Credit requests should be submitted by Distributor within one month after the date of Distributor's invoice for the related transactions.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.5 All credits will be handled as separate financial transactions from the original invoice, and will be evidenced by a written credit request submitted to Tripath. Distributor will not reduce any payment to Tripath to account for anticipated or unauthorized credits (except for short shipments and non-receipt of Products), either at the time of the payment of original invoice, or at the time of providing a credit request. Credit requests should be submitted by Distributor within three months after the date of Distributor's invoice for the related transactions.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in section 7.5 introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the deadline for submitting credit requests. The original text specifies that credit requests should be submitted within 'one month' after the invoice date. The changed text extends this deadline to 'three months'. This creates uncertainty about which deadline is valid, potentially leading to disputes over the acceptance of credit requests.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.5 All credits will be handled as separate financial transactions from the original invoice, and will be evidenced by a written credit request submitted to Tripath. Distributor will not reduce any payment to Tripath to account for anticipated or unauthorized credits (except for short shipments and non-receipt of Products), either at the time of the payment of original invoice, or at the time of providing a credit request. Credit requests should be submitted by Distributor within three months after the date of Distributor's invoice for the related transactions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the deadline for submitting credit requests from one month to three months. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the changed text and the Original Text specifies a different duration for the deadline, this directly contradicts the original deadline. The Perturbation Explanation confirms that this change creates uncertainty about which deadline is valid, potentially leading to disputes over the acceptance of credit requests. This qualifies as an 'Inconsistency' according to the Perturbation Definitions, specifically when 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines...This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.'
